title: Multi-Board Mirror
excerpt: We need an ETA for this.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: We need an ETA for this.

posts.author: iliana.gonzalez (Iliana)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, do you have an ETA of when this feature will be available?

posts.author: jaredt (Jared Tamanaha)
posts.cookedHtml: Greetings, is there any updated ETA on this feature? Mirroring the columns for multiple boards is a must. Thx.

posts.author: Antal (Antal Vig)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can only second this.
As I now noticed that its not possible to link to multiple boards this seems to be falling of the radar.
Would be very handy for a few usecases of my clients…

posts.author: Kerstin (Kerstin Bouguet)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, we also need this.

posts.author: Mitchellcue (Mitchell Cuellar)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this?

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Mitchellcue - This is in limited alpha testing right now with a handful of clients. Some of our clients are on the waiting list and we’re eagerly awaiting when we can test it out and help train our customers on it.

Do you have a connection to an official monday partner? If so, they can advocate on your behalf to try to get this feature earlier (or at least try).

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
@hlopezvc
@iliana.gonzalez
@jaredt
@Antal
@Kerstin
@Mitchellcue

Great news! We’ve been releasing this feature over the past month and I’d love to make sure it’s available for you. Can you share your account URL with me so that I can double-check and add the feature if it’s not already added?

posts.author: Kerstin (Kerstin Bouguet)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am really excited to discover this feature. Our URL is:
r-m-d-company

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: We and many of our clients have been using this for a while now, it works great! One thing to be aware of, however, is that the new update to the Automations that let you trigger from a Mirrored Status change will NOT fully work yet with a multi-mirrored scenario. The Automation will only trigger off values from the first board you linked and mirrored, not any of the others boards. It’s a tricky one, because if you set it up and test but happen to test with a mirrored value from the first board you linked, you’ll think it works just fine. So be aware these two don’t play nicely together, at least not yet.

posts.author: Shouston (Sati Houston)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much @AronK - I have implemented this change and it’s fantastic. It has save me so much time creating duplicate columns.

My only feedback is that currently, if I want to mirror multiple columns (e.g. Timeline and a Numbers column), I need to create multiple Connected Boards+Mirror columns for each. Is this correct? If so, that seems duplicative (and time consuming if I had more than 2 columns or many items to link), as I’m connecting the same boards and items each time, but mirroring different data.

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
You’re welcome, I’m glad it’s helpful!

You should not need to use more than one connect boards column in this scenario. You should be able to connect multiple boards in one connect boards column and then be able to toggle between those boards when linking the items in a cell of that column. If you wish to mirror multiple columns, you would need to add a mirror column for each column you’d like to mirror.

Can you try again with one connect boards column and let me know if you run into any issues?

posts.author: Marc1 (Marc Rosser)
posts.cookedHtml:
Will triggering automations based on mirrored statuses work? I noticed it doesn’t and @PolishedGeek also mentionned it didn’t work completely back in May.

Blockquote
We and many of our clients have been using this for a while now, it works great! One thing to be aware of, however, is that the new update to the Automations that let you trigger from a Mirrored Status change will NOT fully work yet with a multi-mirrored scenario. The Automation will only trigger off values from the first board you linked and mirrored, not any of the others boards. It’s a tricky one, because if you set it up and test but happen to test with a mirrored value from the first board you linked, you’ll think it works just fine. So be aware these two don’t play nicely together, at least not yet.

posts.author: spanaggio8 (Shannon Panaggio)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @AronK,

My team has created an overview board to help us keep up with what our individual departments are doing weekly and I’m trying to figure out if I can duplicate items from everyone’s individual boards so they populate automatically on the overview board in their respective groups (without deleting it from their individual board).

Would this new feature accomplish that? Thank you!

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m glad it works well! At the moment, I do not have an ETA on when this would become available. I believe as a work around you should be able to select all items via batch action and connect the item from the high level board to all of the lower level board items. This should allow you to avoid needing to select them all one by one in the high level connect boards column. Check out this video for more insight > https://www.loom.com/share/fb3bdecb2e854f0b98206fc2707d1c81

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @AronK , thanks for the video, i have tried this workaround but this doesnt work well when you are tryin to connect parent items from many boards to a subitem column with a link and mirror column. When the link column is in a subitem structure, when you try to connect the parent item from another boards to any of the subitems to link them, you will loose reference between the parent item/subitem from the same board.

Example…
If i try to connect items from slave boards to a master board with this structure:

PARENT ITEM 1
SUBITEM1
SUBITEM2

PARENT ITEM 2
SUBITEM1
SUBITEM2

When you try to locate the master board and you see elements to link to, you will see 2 SUBITEM1 and two SUBITEM2 names and you will not know to what parent item they depend to… Our usecase is like this…

posts.author: rsh056 (Richard Helmer)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, I recently tried setting up a multi-board mirror to use with my team, but nobody other than me can see any of the columns in the board. Has anybody else had a similar issue with this?

posts.author: Marc1 (Marc Rosser)
posts.cookedHtml: It has to do with permissions: everyone must have full access to all the boards you are mirroring from. If there’s just one board that doesn’t, then the whole column will be hidden.

posts.author: rsh056 (Richard Helmer)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you, that makes a lot of sense!

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Hmm, great question! I am not certain if this is expected behavior currently vs a potential issue we have to work on. Please send a note to our support team (support@monday.com) so that they can take a look and take appropriate steps from there if needed.

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
